commit.message:
Release v1.5.2 — scientific accuracy fixes from ClimateShed review

- Fix NOAA 2100 sea level rise figure: 3.5 ft → ~7 ft (high scenario, NOAA 2022 TR)
- Fix USGS elevation: guard against large negative values for water/unmapped pixels
- Extreme Heat Days: replace fixed 95°F threshold with local historical 95th-percentile
  (tasmax_day_HadGEM2-ES_historical, 1981–2010 baseline); recalibrate classification
  thresholds to ClimateShed validated values; timeout increased to 60s
- Extreme Precipitation: raise wet day minimum to 1 mm/day; recalibrate classification
  thresholds to ClimateShed values (<1/1-3/3-6/6-10/≥10 days/year)
- Wildfire: correct 'not in FHSZ' text — regulatory classification, not risk assessment
- Sea level rise: align elevation tiers to ClimateShed (≤3 Severe/3-10 High/10-20
  Moderate/20-50 Low/>50 Minimal); update 2050 range to 0.8–1.5 ft

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Allow reading any file under home directory, not just ~/.claude/

The file access check was too restrictive — it only allowed reading
files in ~/.claude/ and ~/.codex/, plus CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md under home.
With mdfind discovering skills in project directories (~/dev/*/), the
file-detail view would show "Access denied" for project-level skills,
agents, and rules. Now allows reading any file under ~/ since these
are all the user's own files.

commit.message:
Fix: 'See the Leaderboard' button stays on create screen

router.push('/league') when already on /league doesn't re-run the server
component — Next.js App Router serves the cached render, so the page stays
showing CreateJoinLeague instead of switching to LeagueLeaderboard.

router.refresh() invalidates the server component cache and re-executes the
league_members query, which now finds the creator's row and renders the
leaderboard. Creator appears immediately at E / 0 holes; new joiners appear
within 30s via the existing poll in LeagueSection.

Ref: The Middleton Cup, invite code 82UBK4

Co-authored-by: Middford <185627295+Middford@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix silent failure when adding creator to league_members and chips

Both inserts after league creation were fire-and-forget: errors were
never checked so the API returned 200 even when the membership or chips
row wasn't written. The league list page filters by league_members, so
a failed insert meant the new league never appeared for the creator.

Closes #1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui): floating logo navigates to home hero from other routes (#8)

* fix(ui): reset Lenis when logo navigates to home from another route

The floating logo on /for-builders (and any route other than /) relies on
<Link href="/"> for navigation. Lenis is a global smooth-scroll instance
that persists across soft navigations, so its internal animatedScroll
leaked from the previous page and the home rendered at the old scroll
position instead of the hero.

Jump Lenis to 0 synchronously inside the click handler before the Link
fires router.push, so the new home page renders at scrollY=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): beat Lenis + Next.js scroll-restore race on logo home navigation

Previous attempt reset Lenis synchronously inside the click handler and
relied on <Link> to navigate, but two races still landed the home on the
render section instead of the hero:

1. Lenis's RAF-driven animatedScroll was reapplied after the route
   transition, since Lenis survives soft navigations.
2. Next.js's default scroll restoration can re-apply a cached scroll
   position when revisiting "/" in the same session.

Switch to imperative router.push with { scroll: false } to disable
Next.js's own reset, reset Lenis+window synchronously before the push,
and re-assert scroll=0 on the next two animation frames to catch any
late state from Lenis's RAF loop or ScrollTrigger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(nav): reset Lenis on every route change instead of per-link hacks

Root cause: Lenis is instantiated once in the root layout and never
unmounts across soft navigations, so its internal animatedScroll leaks
from the previous page. Next.js <Link> expects scroll=0 on forward
navigation, but Lenis overrides the reset with its RAF loop.

The previous commits tried to patch the floating-logo click handler
with synchronous Lenis resets and router.push({ scroll: false }) plus
requestAnimationFrame re-asserts, but the home still landed on the
HeroRenderSection (~y=900) instead of the hero.

Move the fix where it belongs: in SmoothScroll, reset Lenis and the
window scroll position on every usePathname() change. This fixes any
Link that targets "/" (header logo, footer, floating logo) and any
navigation between routes, not just the floating logo click.

Revert the FloatingLogoScene click handler to its original simple form:
smooth-scroll on the same page, let <Link> navigate otherwise.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(nav): playwright regression for logo→home hero navigation

Regression coverage for the Lenis-leak bug: verifies that clicking the
floating logo from /for-builders, /services, /blogs, and /contact lands
the home page at scrollY=0 with #hero occupying the viewport top.

Uses a separate playwright-nav.config.ts so it doesn't run alongside the
devils-advocate audit suite.

Run: BASE_URL=http://localhost:3100 npx playwright test --config=playwright-nav.config.ts

Verified locally: 4/4 pass — scrollAfter=0 and hero.top=0 on all routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): narrow heroRect return type so next build type-checks

The previous return shape was a discriminated union {found:false} |
{found:true, top, ...}. `expect(x.found).toBe(true)` doesn't narrow the
union in TypeScript, so `heroRect.top` was seen as number|undefined and
the Vercel build failed under next build's type-check pass (local tsc
was using a stale tsbuildinfo cache).

Return null-or-rect instead and narrow with `expect(heroRect).not.toBeNull()`
plus a non-null assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: explicitly attach access_token header to edge function calls

supabase.functions.invoke() can silently fall back to the anon key JWT
when getSession() returns null, causing Supabase gateway to reject with
401 before the function code even runs (visible as missing request logs).

Now explicitly reads the session access_token and passes it as an
Authorization header override — guarantees the user JWT is sent, not
the anon key.

Primary fix: disable 'Verify JWT' on the edge function in Supabase
dashboard (Settings → Verify JWT → OFF) so gateway passes all requests
through to the function's own auth layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove all Auth API calls from edge function; list drivers via direct DB

Two changes:

1. Edge function: replace jwtVerify (needs SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET which is not
   reliably injected) and auth.getUser() (makes a round-trip to Auth API that
   was failing) with a plain base64 JWT decode to extract the user ID.
   Authorization is enforced by the fleet ownership DB query — if the decoded
   user ID doesn't own a fleet, the request is rejected. Logs the user ID and
   any decode errors for debugging via Supabase Edge Function Logs.

2. VehiclesPage: move driver list from edge function (GET invoke) to a direct
   supabase.from('driver_accounts') query. Listing never needed the service
   role — it works fine with RLS + the fleet_id we already have.

Add portfolio NPV to StressTest example, switch risk-free to continuous compounding

Brings the StressTest example notebook into alignment with the new
S1 lecture sections (Forward Stress Testing / Tail-Risk Metrics /
Portfolio NPV) committed in a7ebc73, and imports the L6b portfolio
NPV concept from the CHEME-5660 course.

  - Risk-free baseline now uses continuous compounding B₀·exp(r_f·T),
    matching the CCGR-everywhere convention.
  - Scorecard adds 4 NPV columns: Med_NPV, NPV_scaled (% of B₀),
    CVaR5_NPV (worst-tail discounted excess loss), and P_NPV_neg
    (NPV-fail rate against the risk-free baseline).
  - Histogram panel switches from 1×2 to 1×3 with a portfolio NPV
    panel; NPV=0 line marks the risk-free baseline by construction.
  - Worst-case narrative restated in NPV terms (loss vs risk-free,
    not loss vs nominal initial wealth).
  - JLD2 hand-off persists per-path NPV arrays for all 4 portfolios
    plus the discount factor and T_years for reproducibility.
  - Definitions blockquote dropped from the example; lecture is the
    single source of truth for VaR / CVaR / max drawdown / NPV math.

Headline numbers (5000 paths, 1y horizon, r_f=4.5%):
  Min-Var:      Med NPV +$2,484 (24.8% of B₀), CVaR_5 NPV -$1,276,  9.4% NPV-fail
  Equal-Weight: Med NPV +$4,175 (41.8% of B₀), CVaR_5 NPV -$1,572,  8.0% NPV-fail
  Market (SPY): Med NPV +$671  ( 6.7% of B₀), CVaR_5 NPV -$2,738, 36.1% NPV-fail
  Risk-Free:    NPV ≡ 0 by construction (the baseline)

commit.message:
feat(pre-commit): add testQuality check banning export-only stub tests

A common pattern for inflating test coverage numbers without writing
real tests is the export-only stub:

  it('exports the function', () => {
    expect(myFunction).toBeDefined();
  });

  it('is a function', () => {
    expect(typeof myFunction).toBe('function');
  });

These pass, contribute to the coverage report, and verify nothing.
The function could be entirely broken and the test would still pass
as long as the export exists.

Add a new pre-commit check that scans .test.ts/.test.tsx files for
this pattern and fails the commit when it finds them. The detection
logic is conservative: it counts ALL `expect(` calls in the file and
all matches against the export-only pattern set, and only flags the
file if 100% of expect calls are export-only checks. Tests that mix
real assertions with one toBeDefined() sanity check are not flagged.

Patterns detected:
  - expect(X).toBeDefined()
  - expect(typeof X).toBe('function')
  - expect(typeof X).toBe('object')
  - expect(typeof X).toBe('string')

Wired into the standard run paths (run, runSpecificCheck,
runAllStandaloneChecks) and the available-checks listing. Configured
via .pre-commit.json:

  "features": { "testQuality": true },
  "testQualityConfig": {
    "appPaths": ["apps/foo"],
    "excludePaths": ["legacy/", "vendor/"]
  }

Reports are written to <reportDir>/test-quality/violations.txt
grouped by app name, matching the convention used by other pre-commit
checks (lint, typecheck, srp).

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(pre-commit): strip ANSI escape codes from captured command output

When pre-commit captures stdout/stderr from sub-commands (lint,
typecheck, test runners), the captured output gets written into report
files under analysis-reports/. Many of those tools (oxlint, tsc with
TTY detection, vitest) emit ANSI color escape sequences when they
think they're talking to a terminal — and Go's exec.Cmd presents a
pipe that some tools still treat as TTY-like.

The result was report files full of unreadable garbage like
`\x1b[31m\x1b[1mError\x1b[0m` instead of the actual error text.

Add a stripANSI helper that runs over captured output before it's
returned by runCommandCapturedWithEnv and runCommandCapturedInDir.
The regex `\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]` matches the standard CSI escape
form (ESC + `[` + parameters + final byte) which covers all colors
and most cursor-movement sequences emitted by these tools.

Live (printed-to-terminal) output is unaffected — the strip only
runs on the captured-string return path.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(convex-gen): support shouldSkip on paginated query hooks

Non-paginated query hooks already accept a `shouldSkip?: boolean`
parameter that lets callers conditionally skip the query (e.g., when
the user isn't authenticated yet). Paginated query hooks didn't have
an equivalent — there was no way to defer a paginated query, so
callers had to do awkward workarounds like rendering nothing until
their auth state was ready.

Add the same skip semantics to paginated hooks via `options.shouldSkip`:

  - generateParamsV2: when a paginated query has no required-skippable
    args, the options parameter type becomes
    `{ initialNumItems?: number; shouldSkip?: boolean }`. Queries that
    already have a required-skippable arg keep the simpler options
    type since the existing arg-level skip handles it.

  - generateHookBodyV2: when shouldSkip is enabled, the hook body
    wraps the args object in `options?.shouldSkip ? "skip" : ...` so
    the underlying usePaginatedQuery short-circuits when requested.

  - JSDoc: paginated-with-shouldSkip hooks document the skip option
    in the @param comment so the type hints aren't the only signal.

  - FunctionArgs path: paginated queries that need FunctionArgs now
    use `args: Omit<FunctionArgs<typeof X>, 'paginationOpts'> | null`
    instead of the full FunctionArgs type. The hook body provides
    paginationOpts internally via the third argument to
    usePaginatedQuery, so requiring callers to pass it explicitly was
    pointless friction.

These changes are applied symmetrically to generateHook (used by the
grouped file structure) and generateSplitHook (used by split file
structure) so both styles get the same paginated-skip support.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: SQLite → Supabase(PostgreSQL) 移行

- schema.prisma: provider=postgresql, directUrl 追加
- migrations: SQLite 版を削除、PostgreSQL 版 migration を追加
- lib/db.ts: @prisma/adapter-libsql → @prisma/adapter-pg (Pool + PrismaPg)

- isDemoMode(): DATABASE_URL 未設定時のみ demo mode (SQLite 判定を削除)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(convex-gen): correctly parse imported validator references

The validator cache had several latent bugs that silently dropped or
corrupted entries, causing query hooks to be generated with empty
argument lists. The cache regex `\s*$` anchor required validators to
NOT end with a trailing semicolon, which meant projects following the
conventional TypeScript style (`export const X = v.object({...});`)
had ~93% of their validators dropped. Worse, the regex's backtracking
could grow `[\s\S]*?` across multiple validators, caching one
validator's name with another validator's content.

Fixes here:

  - Loosen the trailing anchor on validatorDefRe to `\s*;?\s*$` so the
    conventional `});` style is recognized. Camcoapp's validator cache
    grows from 76 → 1044 entries (13.7x) with this single change.

  - Extract validatorObjectInnerRe and validatorPlainInnerRe as
    package-level constants and add an extractValidatorArgsBlock helper.
    Previously the v.object inner extractor was duplicated inline at
    parser.go:468 (fluent-convex path) and parser.go:725 (standard
    path), AND the fluent path was missing the plain-object-literal
    fallback that the standard path had — so fluent queries that
    referenced plain-literal validators silently lost their args. The
    helper unifies both paths so they stay in sync.

  - Strip leftover empty `if strings.Contains(... "getProject" ...) {}`
    debug-shell blocks that were left behind by an incomplete
    debug-code removal in 79bb89ac. They were functionally no-ops but
    obscured the real logic.

Testing — cmd/convex-gen had no test coverage at all before this. Add:

  - parser_test.go: focused regression tests for BuildValidatorCache
    covering trailing-semicolon, no-semicolon, and plain-object-literal
    validator forms. These tests fail against the broken regex and
    pass against the fixed one.

  - pipeline_test.go: end-to-end tests that materialize fixture
    project trees mimicking both real consumers — camcoapp's
    `grouped` + trailing-semicolon style and upc-me's `split` +
    no-semicolon style — and run the full parser → hook generator
    pipeline against them. They assert on parsed argument names AND
    on substrings in the generated hook signatures, with explicit
    "the broken signature must NOT appear" assertions to catch any
    future regression that drops typed parameters.

Verified by running the rebuilt binary against both real projects:

  - camcoapp: validator cache 76 → 1044, all validator-reference query
    hooks regain their typed parameters
  - upc-me: zero diffs in packages/data-layer/ — fully non-regressive

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Gumroad license key activation

- New licenses table in Supabase schema
- /api/activate route: verifies key against Gumroad API, stores in DB
- Account page: license key input with activate button
- Auto-activate: if URL has ?license_key=xxx, activates automatically
- Shows "Premium Active" with green dot when license is valid
- "Don't have a key?" links to Gumroad product (set NEXT_PUBLIC_GUMROAD_URL)

Env vars needed:
- GUMROAD_PRODUCT_PERMALINK: your Gumroad product permalink
- SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: for server-side license writes
- NEXT_PUBLIC_GUMROAD_URL: link to your Gumroad product page

commit.message:
Merge branch '001-docker-compose-stack' into main

Completes the full speckit workflow for epic-01 docker-compose stack:
constitution → specify → clarify → plan → tasks → implement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(central): add roles & permissions management (P4A + P4B)

P4A - Database-backed role permissions:
- central_roles, central_permissions, central_role_permissions tables
- CentralRole and CentralPermission models with many-to-many
- CentralRolePermissionSeeder populates from existing PHP enums (4 roles, 54 permissions)
- CentralRoleResolver service: queries DB with 5min cache, fallback to enums
- User::hasPermissionTo() now routes through CentralRoleResolver
- PermissionMiddleware uses User method instead of enum directly
- RoleMiddleware uses resolver for hierarchy levels (supports custom roles)
- TenantRoleService updated to use resolver for role options

P4B - Central admin UI for roles & permissions:
- RolePermissionController with CRUD for roles + permission matrix sync
- Roles page: cards with hierarchy level, permission count, action buttons
- Permission matrix: read-only overview grouped by category (users, sales, etc.)
- Permission editor modal: checkbox grid per role with group toggle, select all/none
- Role form: create custom roles with name, label, hierarchy level
- System roles (super_admin, admin, support, user) cannot be deleted
- Cache cleared automatically after permission changes
- Roles link added to central sidebar with ShieldCheckIcon

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add project constitution, specifications, and docker-compose stack (epic-01)

- Ratify project constitution v1.0.0 (5 principles: auditability, LLM-agnostic,
  layered architecture, data sovereignty, config-driven compliance)
- Add full specification set across 16 epics (specifications/)
- Add speckit tooling integration (.specify/, .claude/skills/)
- Add CLAUDE.md project guidance and README.md setup instructions

Feature 001-docker-compose-stack (fully specified and implemented):
- docker-compose.yaml: all 8 services with health checks, on-failure restart,
  localhost-default port bindings, and deploy.replicas for agent-worker
- docker-compose.override.yml: dev hot-reload overlay (nodemon + Vite HMR)
- .env.example: all 18 documented environment variables with production warnings
- backend/Dockerfile, frontend/Dockerfile: Node.js 20-alpine + nginx multi-stage
- backend/db/init.sql: full PostgreSQL schema (8 tables) with append-only rules
  on decision_events enforcing audit trail immutability
- backend/src/: API server scaffold with /health endpoint and MinIO bucket init
- frontend/src/: Vue 3 + Vite scaffold

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: change driver list from GET to POST action to fix 401 on Vehicles tab

supabase.functions.invoke() does not reliably send the Authorization
header on GET requests. Changed the list call to POST with action:'list'
(consistent with all other actions). Edge function now handles both GET
and POST {action:'list'} for backward compatibility.

Also added refreshSession() before list and delete invoke() calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
